title: Unauthorized File Download
description: Sensitive documents were accessed from a shared workstation. Analyse browsing history and download artefacts to determine the sequence of events.

events.description: Bing search: "meridian corp intranet document library"
events.explanation: The session started with a targeted search for the internal document repository.

relationships.label: The search result led directly to the corporate intranet landing page.
A targeted intranet search commonly precedes a session cookie being set.

hints: Users often start from a search before navigating to a known internal portal.
A successful intranet visit frequently leads to authentication artefacts such as session cookies.

events.description: Visited https://intranet.meridiancorp.local/ (title: Meridian Corp Intranet Home)
events.explanation: The browser accessed the corporate intranet landing page from the search result.

relationships.label: Loading an authenticated portal page commonly precedes session cookie creation.

hints: A successful intranet visit frequently leads to authentication artefacts such as session cookies.
The landing page is often the first step before deeper authenticated navigation.

events.description: Cookie set: SSO_SESSION on intranet.meridiancorp.local (HttpOnly, expires 2024-10-22T20:33:42Z)
events.explanation: Single sign-on authentication established a session cookie, confirming the user was logged into the intranet.

relationships.label: Single sign-on authentication typically follows reaching the authenticated portal.
Authenticated sessions often enable subsequent protected internal searches.

hints: Session cookies usually appear after a successful authentication event.
An active session cookie can indicate the user was able to access protected internal pages.

events.explanation: An internal search for confidential financial material indicates deliberate targeting of sensitive files.

relationships.label: An internal search for sensitive material commonly precedes opening the exact report.

hints: A sensitive file search often reflects a deliberate intent to locate restricted material.
Searches for confidential documents frequently precede the targeted document page being opened.

events.explanation: The search returned the restricted finance report page in the browser history.

relationships.label: Opening a report page frequently precedes downloading the document.

hints: Opening a report page is often the precursor to a local download.
Restricted finance reports are frequently downloaded after being accessed in a browser.

events.description: Downloaded Q3_2024_Financial_Report_CONFIDENTIAL.pdf to C:\Users\SharedWS\Downloads\ (1.8 MB, Chrome)
events.explanation: The confidential PDF was saved locally, creating a key exfiltration artefact on the shared workstation.

relationships.label: Saving a document often precedes attempts to move or share it elsewhere.
Downloading sensitive material often precedes cloud transfer or sharing activity.

hints: A local download can indicate preparation for later transfer or exfiltration.
The order of download and cloud activity can indicate intent and execution sequence.

events.explanation: Shortly after the download, the user opened a cloud storage service, suggesting intent to upload or transfer the file.

relationships.label: Opening cloud storage shortly after a sensitive download can suggest transfer intent.
Visiting cloud storage can precede cookie refresh during account use.

hints: Opening cloud storage shortly after a sensitive download can suggest transfer intent.
Cloud home pages are often opened before authenticated upload activity.

events.explanation: An active Google session cookie confirms the cloud storage account was already authenticated during the transfer window.

relationships.label: Cloud storage cookie refresh commonly follows visiting the service.

hints: A refreshed cloud session cookie suggests active authenticated use of the service.
Cloud session activity is often authenticated during or shortly after opening the cloud home page.
